docs.title: Welcome to Documentation
docs.text:
Your single source of truth for planning, requirements, technical, and delivery documentation.

  • Agile — Epics, stories, BRDs, and sprint reports
  • Technical — APIs, architecture, ADRs, and runbooks
  • User Guides — Getting started, features, and FAQ
  • Templates — Start any document from a consistent baseline

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Getting Started — New here? Start here
  • Latest Release — What's new
  • Sprint Report — Current sprint status

docs.title: Documentation Principles
docs.text:
1. Single source of truth — If it's a plan, requirement, or decision, it lives here
  2. Traceable — Business need → BRD → Epic → Story → Release
  3. Reviewed — Changes go through pull requests
  4. Versioned — Every edit is kept forever

docs.title: Our Platform
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    Client[Client / Customer] -->|API| Gateway[API Gateway]
    Gateway --> Services[Domain Services]
    Services --> Data[(Data Layer)]
    Services --> Risk[Risk & Compliance]
    Data --> Notify[Notifications]

docs.title: Agile Documentation
docs.text:
Epics, user stories, BRDs, release notes, and sprint reports.

  • Epics — Large bodies of work
  • User Stories — Deliverable slices of value
  • BRDs — Business requirements
  • Release Notes — What shipped

docs.title: Business Requirements Documents (BRDs)
docs.text: A BRD captures the business problem, objectives, stakeholders, and requirements agreed with the business before solution work begins. It is the anchor that epics and stories trace back to.

docs.title: Published BRDs
docs.text:
ID Title Status Author Related epic BRD-2026-014 Instant Payments Approved S. Chen (BA) EPIC-001

New BRDs start from the BRD template.

docs.title: BRD-2026-014 · Instant Payments
docs.text: Field Value Status Approved Author S. Chen (Business Analyst) Approvers Head of Payments, Risk & Compliance, Partner Success Version 1.2 Related epic EPIC-001 · Instant Payments

docs.title: 1. Executive summary
docs.text: Partner banks require the ability to offer their customers instant, 24/7 account-to-account payments. Today Helios settles transfers in batches with delays of up to several hours, causing customer dissatisfaction and lost volume to competitors on instant rails. This BRD defines the business requirements for a real-time payments capability.

docs.title: 2. Business objectives
docs.text: # Objective Measure of success O1 Reduce settlement time 95th-percentile settlement < 10 seconds O2 Grow transfer volume +18% transfer volume within two quarters of launch O3 Reduce support load −30% "where is my money" tickets O4 Contain fraud risk Fraud loss ≤ 5 bps of transferred value

docs.title: 3. Stakeholders
docs.text: Role Interest Head of Payments Sponsor; owns the business case Partner banks Consumers of the API; want reliability and speed Risk & Compliance Fraud controls, regulatory reporting Customer Support Fewer, simpler tickets

docs.title: 4. Scope
docs.text:
In scope: domestic instant transfers between Helios-connected accounts, via API and web console, with real-time status.

Out of scope: cross-border/FX, bulk disbursements, card transactions.

docs.title: 5. Business requirements
docs.text: ID Requirement Priority BR-1 Payments settle in under 10 seconds, 24/7/365. Must BR-2 Each payment returns a real-time status (settled / held / failed). Must BR-3 Duplicate submissions must not move funds twice. Must BR-4 Every payment is screened for fraud before settlement. Must BR-5 Per-account and per-transaction limits are configurable. Should BR-6 Partners receive a webhook on status change. Should

docs.title: 6. Assumptions & constraints
docs.text:
• Partner banks integrate over the existing authenticated Helios API.
  • Instant settlement is irreversible; fraud prevention must be pre-settlement.
  • Must comply with applicable real-time payment scheme rules.

docs.title: 7. Non-functional requirements
docs.text:
• Availability: 99.95% for the payments endpoint.
  • Performance: < 10 s settlement at p95; fraud score < 500 ms at p99.
  • Auditability: every payment and decision is logged and retained per policy.

docs.title: 8. Acceptance & sign-off
docs.text:
This BRD is considered met when EPIC-001 is accepted against requirements BR-1 through BR-6 and non-functional targets are demonstrated in load testing.

Approver Decision Date Head of Payments Approved 2026-06-30 Risk & Compliance Approved 2026-07-02

docs.title: Epics
docs.text: An epic is a large body of work that delivers a distinct business capability and is broken down into user stories. Epics typically span multiple sprints.

docs.title: Active epics
docs.text: ID Title Status Target release Owner EPIC-001 Instant Payments In progress v2.4.0 A. Rivera (PO) EPIC-002 Fraud & Risk Screening In progress v2.5.0 A. Rivera (PO)

docs.title: Epic lifecycle
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Draft --> Refined --> Committed --> InProgress[In progress] --> Done

New epics start from the epic template.

docs.title: EPIC-001 · Instant Payments
docs.text: Field Value Status In progress Owner A. Rivera (Product Owner) Target release v2.4.0 Source BRD BRD · Instant Payments Projects board Helios Delivery → filter epic:EPIC-001

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Enable Helios customers to send and receive account-to-account payments that settle in under 10 seconds, 24/7, through the partner-bank API and the web console.

docs.title: Business value
docs.text: Partner banks lose transfer volume to competitors offering instant rails. Delivering sub-10-second settlement is expected to increase transfer volume by 18% and reduce support tickets related to "where is my money" by 30%.

docs.title: Goals & success metrics
docs.text:
• 95th-percentile settlement time < 10 seconds.
  • 99.95% availability for the payments endpoint.
  • Zero increase in fraud loss rate versus standard transfers (guarded by EPIC-002).

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
In scope

  • Domestic instant transfers between Helios-connected accounts.
  • Idempotent payment API with real-time status webhooks.
  • Console UI for initiating and tracking a payment.

Out of scope

  • Cross-border / FX payments (future epic).
  • Bulk / batch disbursements.

docs.title: Child user stories
docs.text: ID Title Status HEL-142 Send an instant payment via API In progress HEL-143 Receive payment status webhook To do HEL-144 Track payment in web console To do HEL-145 Idempotency & retry handling To do

docs.title: Dependencies & risks
docs.text:
Dependencies

  • Ledger service must expose a synchronous reservation call (owned by Platform team).
  • Fraud engine (EPIC-002) must return a score within 500 ms to stay inside the 10-second budget.

docs.title: Definition of Done
docs.text:
• All child stories accepted.
  • Non-functional targets (latency, availability) verified in load test.
  • Runbook and release notes published.
  • Signed off by Business Analyst against the BRD.

docs.title: EPIC-002 · Fraud & Risk Screening
docs.text: Field Value Status In progress Owner KASS (Product Owner) Target release v2.5.0 Projects board Helios Delivery → filter epic:EPIC-002

docs.title: Summary
docs.text: Screen every payment in real time with a machine-learning risk score, blocking or holding suspicious transactions without adding meaningful latency to the instant-payments flow.

docs.title: Business value
docs.text: Instant settlement removes the ability to claw back fraudulent transfers, so prevention must happen before settlement. Target: keep fraud loss below 5 basis points of transferred value while holding false-positive rate under 1.5%.

docs.title: Goals & success metrics
docs.text:
• Risk score returned in < 500 ms at the 99th percentile.
  • Fraud loss rate ≤ 0.05% of value.
  • False-positive (good payments held) rate ≤ 1.5%.

docs.title: Scope
docs.text:
In scope

  • Real-time scoring API called synchronously by the payments service.
  • Rules + ML hybrid model with a human-review queue for held payments.
  • Analyst dashboard for reviewing and releasing holds.

Out of scope

  • Chargeback / dispute management (handled by a separate domain).

docs.title: Child user stories
docs.text: ID Title Status HEL-158 Return real-time fraud score In progress HEL-159 Hold-and-review queue To do HEL-160 Analyst review dashboard To do

docs.title: Dependencies & risks
docs.text:
Key risk

The 500 ms scoring budget is tight. If the model can't meet it, the payments service falls back to a rules-only score and flags the transaction for post-settlement review.

docs.title: Definition of Done
docs.text:
• All child stories accepted.
  • Latency budget verified under peak load.
  • Model performance signed off by Risk & Compliance.
  • Release notes and analyst runbook published.

docs.title: Meeting Notes
docs.text: A record of planning sessions, key ceremonies, and decisions. Meeting notes keep decisions discoverable and traceable.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
Meeting Date PI Planning — Q3 2026-06-26

New notes start from the meeting notes template.

docs.title: PI Planning — Q3 2026
docs.text: Field Value Date 2026-06-26 Facilitator D. Novak (Scrum Master) Participants Helios team, PO, BA, Risk & Compliance, Platform team Program increment Q3 (Sprints 42–47)

docs.title: PI objectives
docs.text: # Objective Confidence 1 Instant Payments API to GA High 2 Real-time fraud screening live Medium 3 Partner sandbox environment Medium

docs.title: Committed epics
docs.text:
• EPIC-001 · Instant Payments — target v2.4.0.
  • EPIC-002 · Fraud & Risk Screening — target v2.5.0.

docs.title: Key decisions
docs.text: # Decision Rationale D1 Fraud scoring is called synchronously within the payment flow Instant settlement is irreversible; screening must be pre-settlement D2 500 ms scoring budget with a rules-only fallback Protects the 10-second settlement SLA if the ML model is slow D3 Deprecate /v1/transfers Consolidate on the v2 payments API

docs.title: Risks & dependencies
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Ledger[Ledger sync reservation] --> EPIC001[EPIC-001]
    Fraud[Fraud engine < 500ms] --> EPIC001
    EPIC002[EPIC-002] --> Fraud

docs.title: Action items
docs.text: Action Owner Due Confirm ledger sync API contract Platform team Sprint 42 Draft partner sandbox proposal A. Rivera Sprint 43

docs.title: Release Notes
docs.text:
Release notes record what shipped, when, and what changed for each version of the Helios Payments Platform. They are the human-readable companion to the technical changelog and GitHub Releases.

How this maps to GitHub

On rollout, each of these pages corresponds to a GitHub Release tagged in the repo (e.g. v2.4.0). GitHub Releases can also carry build artifacts and packages — the equivalent of publishing to JFrog Artifactory.

docs.title: Releases
docs.text:
Version Date Highlights Status v2.4.0 2026-07-17 Instant Payments (API) GA Released v2.3.0 2026-06-19 Console redesign, webhook retries Released

New release notes start from the release note template.

docs.title: Release v2.3.0
docs.text: Field Value Release date 2026-06-19 Release manager L. Fischer Environment Production

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text: A refreshed web console and more resilient webhook delivery.

docs.title: New features
docs.text:
• Redesigned payments console with improved accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA).
  • Webhook automatic retries with exponential backoff.

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text:
• Faster transaction search (average query time down 55%).
  • Expanded audit log detail for compliance exports.

docs.title: Fixes
docs.text:
• Corrected timezone handling in scheduled reports.
  • Fixed a session timeout that logged users out prematurely.

docs.title: Upgrade notes
docs.text: No action required. Webhook consumers should ensure endpoints are idempotent to handle retries.

docs.title: Release v2.4.0
docs.text: Field Value Release date 2026-07-17 Release manager L. Fischer Epic delivered EPIC-001 · Instant Payments Environment Production

docs.title: Highlights
docs.text: Instant Payments is now generally available over the partner-bank API. Customers can move funds between Helios-connected accounts with settlement in under 10 seconds, 24/7.

docs.title: New features
docs.text:
• Instant payments API (POST /v2/payments) with idempotency support — HEL-142.
  • Real-time status webhooks for payment lifecycle events.
  • Configurable per-account limits in the web console.

docs.title: Improvements
docs.text:
• Ledger reservation call is now synchronous, cutting settlement latency by ~40%.
  • Clearer API error codes (INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS, PAYMENT_HELD).

docs.title: Fixes
docs.text:
• Resolved a rounding discrepancy in multi-currency balance display.
  • Fixed webhook delivery ordering under high load.

docs.title: Known issues
docs.text: • Web console tracking view (HEL-144) ships in v2.4.1; use the API status endpoint until then.

docs.title: Upgrade notes
docs.text: No breaking changes. The /v1/transfers endpoint remains available but is deprecated — migrate to /v2/payments before v3.0.

docs.title: Traceability
docs.text: Delivered stories: HEL-142. Screened by fraud scoring pilot from EPIC-002.

docs.title: Reports
docs.text: Sprint and program-level reports capture outcomes, metrics, and decisions from our agile ceremonies.

docs.title: Reports
docs.text:
Report Sprint Date Sprint 42 Review 42 2026-07-16 Sprint 42 Retrospective 42 2026-07-16

Live metrics vs. static reports

These pages are the narrative record. For live burndown, velocity, and status, use the GitHub Projects board and its built-in Insights charts — the equivalent of Jira dashboards.

docs.title: Sprint 42 Retrospective
docs.text: Field Value Sprint 42 Facilitator D. Novak (Scrum Master) Format Start / Stop / Continue

docs.title: What went well (Continue)
docs.text:
• Pairing on the ledger integration unblocked HEL-142 quickly.
  • Daily 15-minute fraud-latency check kept HEL-158 on track.
  • Clear Definition of Done meant no story bounced back in review.

docs.title: What to improve (Stop)
docs.text:
• Late scope addition mid-sprint pushed one story to carry-over.
  • Test environment instability cost roughly half a day.

docs.title: What to try (Start)
docs.text:
• Freeze sprint scope after day 2; new requests go to the backlog.
  • Add a pre-sprint environment health check.

docs.title: Action items
docs.text:
Action Owner Due Document scope-freeze policy in team norms D. Novak Sprint 43, day 1 Automate environment health check J. Okoro Sprint 43 Raise sandbox-environment request with platform team A. Rivera 2026-07-20

Note

Action items are tracked as issues on the GitHub Projects board and linked here so the retro's outcomes don't get lost.

docs.title: Sprint 42 Review
docs.text: Field Value Sprint 42 (2026-07-03 → 2026-07-16) Sprint goal Ship Instant Payments API to GA readiness Facilitator D. Novak (Scrum Master) Attendees Team, PO, 2 partner-bank stakeholders

docs.title: Sprint goal outcome
docs.text: Met. The instant payments API reached GA readiness and shipped in v2.4.0.

docs.title: Committed vs. completed
docs.text: Metric Value Committed points 34 Completed points 31 Velocity (3-sprint avg) 30 Stories accepted 6 of 7

docs.title: Demonstrated
docs.text:
• HEL-142 — live instant payment settling in 6.2 s. Accepted.
  • Real-time webhook status stream. Accepted.
  • Fraud scoring pilot (HEL-158) — in progress, demoed at 480 ms p99. Carried to Sprint 43.

docs.title: Stakeholder feedback
docs.text:
• Partner banks asked for a sandbox environment to test integrations — logged as a new backlog item.
  • Request to expose settlement time in the webhook payload — accepted, small change.

docs.title: Carry-over
docs.text: • HEL-158 (3 points remaining) moves to Sprint 43.

docs.title: User Stories
docs.text: A user story is a small, independently valuable slice of functionality that can be delivered within a sprint. Stories roll up to epics.

docs.title: Story format
docs.text:
As a \<role>, I want \<capability>, so that \<benefit>.

Each story carries acceptance criteria in Given / When / Then form and a clear Definition of Done.

docs.title: Current stories
docs.text:
ID Title Epic Points Status HEL-142 Send an instant payment via API EPIC-001 5 In progress HEL-158 Return real-time fraud score EPIC-002 8 In progress

New stories start from the user story template.

docs.title: HEL-142 · Send an instant payment via API
docs.text: Field Value Issue HEL-142 · Send Instant Payment Epic EPIC-001 · Instant Payments Sprint 42 Estimate 5 points Status In progress Assignee J. Okoro

docs.title: Story
docs.text: As a partner-bank integrator, I want to submit a payment through a single API call, so that my customers' funds move to the beneficiary in under 10 seconds.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
```gherkin Scenario: Successful instant payment Given a funded source account and a valid beneficiary When the client POSTs a payment to /v2/payments with a unique idempotency key Then the API responds 201 with status "SETTLED" And the settlement completes in under 10 seconds And the ledger reflects the debit and credit atomically

Scenario: Insufficient funds Given a source account without enough balance When the client POSTs the payment Then the API responds 422 with error code "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS" And no ledger entry is created

Scenario: Duplicate request Given a payment was already submitted with idempotency key K When the client resubmits with key K Then the API returns the original result without creating a second payment ```

docs.title: Notes & decisions
docs.text:
• Payment amounts are validated against per-account limits configured in the console.
  • The fraud score (see HEL-158) is called synchronously; a score above the block threshold returns 409 PAYMENT_HELD.

docs.title: Definition of Done
docs.text:
• Code merged and covered by unit + contrac.
  • API reference updated.
  • Load test confirms < 10 s at target throughput.
  • Demoed at Sprint 42 Review.

docs.title: HEL-158 · Return real-time fraud score
docs.text: Field Value Issue HEL-158 · Return real-time fraud score Epic EPIC-002 · Fraud & Risk Screening Sprint 42 Estimate 8 points Status In progress Assignee M. Haddad

docs.title: Story
docs.text: As a payments service, I want a fraud risk score for each transaction in under 500 ms, so that I can block or hold suspicious payments before they settle.

docs.title: Acceptance criteria
docs.text:
```gherkin Scenario: Low-risk payment Given a transaction with normal characteristics When the payments service requests a score from /v2/risk/score Then a score between 0 and 100 is returned in under 500 ms And a score below 40 results in decision "ALLOW"

Scenario: High-risk payment Given a transaction matching a high-risk pattern When a score is requested Then a score above 80 results in decision "BLOCK" And the transaction is added to the review queue

Scenario: Scoring timeout fallback Given the ML model does not respond within 500 ms When a score is requested Then a rules-only score is returned And the transaction is flagged for post-settlement review ```

docs.title: Notes & decisions
docs.text:
• Decision thresholds (ALLOW / HOLD / BLOCK) are configurable by Risk & Compliance without a code change.
  • Every scoring call is logged for model monitoring and auditing.

docs.title: Definition of Done
docs.text:
• Model + rules engine deployed behind the scoring API.
  • p99 latency < 500 ms verified.
  • Fallback path tested.
  • Signed off by Risk & Compliance.

docs.title: Templates
docs.text: Copy-paste starting points so every document of a given type looks the same. Consistency makes docs faster to write and easier to read.

docs.title: How to use a template
docs.text:
1. Open the template you need and copy its contents.
  2. Create a new file in the matching folder (see Getting Started).
  3. Fill in the placeholders in <angle brackets> and delete the guidance notes.
  4. Propose the change as a pull request.

docs.title: Available templates
docs.text:
• Epic template
  • User story template
  • BRD template
  • Release note template
  • Meeting notes template

docs.title: 📘 Playbook Batch Processing v1.1.0 — Resumen Ejecutivo y Estándar de Desarrollo
docs.text: Objetivo: Definir el estándar para diseñar, desarrollar y operar procesos Batch en Spring Batch garantizando idempotencia, resiliencia, escalabilidad, observabilidad y recuperación ante fallas. 1

docs.title: Principios Fundamentales
docs.text:
Todo proceso Batch debe ser:

  • ✅ Idempotente
  • ✅ Transaccional
  • ✅ Resiliente
  • ✅ Autónomo
  • ✅ Escalable
  • ✅ Monitoreable
  • ✅ Recuperable ante fallos
  • ✅ Modular
  • ✅ Eficiente en el uso de recursos

Estos principios constituyen la base de cualquier implementación Batch corporativa. 1

docs.title: 🏛 Arquitectura Conceptual
docs.text:
flowchart LR

JOB[Job]

JOB --> STEP1[Step]
JOB --> STEP2[Step]
JOB --> STEPN[Step N]

STEP1 --> READER[ItemReader]
READER --> PROCESSOR[ItemProcessor]
PROCESSOR --> WRITER[ItemWriter]

JOB --> LISTENER[Listeners]
JOB --> META[Metadata]

docs.title: 1. Idempotencia Obligatoria
docs.text: Un mismo Job ejecutado varias veces con los mismos parámetros debe producir exactamente el mismo resultado. 1

docs.title: Ejemplos Permitidos
docs.text:
• Generación de reportes
  • Consultas
  • Notificaciones
  • Validaciones

docs.title: Ejemplos No Permitidos
docs.text:
• Duplicación de liquidaciones
  • Duplicación de facturación
  • Reprocesamiento financiero sin control

docs.title: 2. Monitoreo Obligatorio
docs.text:
Todo Job debe registrar:

Evento Información requerida Inicio Nombre del proceso Inicio Parámetros recibidos Fin Tiempo total Fin Registros procesados Fin Archivos generados Error Excepción ocurrida Error Registro afectado

1

docs.title: 3. Ejecución Fuera de Horario Operativo
docs.text:
Los Batch consumen:

  • CPU
  • RAM
  • Red
  • Base de datos

Por ello deben ejecutarse preferentemente fuera de horarios productivos. 1

docs.title: Incorrecto
docs.text: sql SELECT * FROM CLIENTES;

docs.title: Correcto
docs.text:
sql SELECT ID_CLIENTE,NOMBRE FROM CLIENTES;

Principio:

text Menos datos = menor consumo de recursos1

docs.title: 5. Control de Integridad
docs.text:
Todo proceso debe responder:

text ¿Cuántos registros entraron? ¿Cuántos se leyeron? ¿Cuántos se procesaron? ¿Cuántos se escribieron? ¿Cuántos fallaron?

1

docs.title: 📋 Clasificación de Procesos Batch
docs.text: Tipo Objetivo Procesamiento de datos Aplicar reglas de negocio Procesamiento de archivos CSV, TXT, Excel Utilerías Kafka, integraciones Consultas BD Reportes e informes

docs.title: Permitidos
docs.text:
• Lectura
  • Reportes
  • Notificaciones
  • Utilerías
  • Validaciones
  • Procesamiento no crítico

docs.title: Estrategias
docs.text:
• Retry
  • Reprocesamiento completo
  • Reproceso parcial
flowchart LR
A[Error]
--> B[Retry]
--> C[Reintento]
```[1](https://onuris-my.sharepoint.com/personal/1201791_onuriscp_com/Documents/Archivos%20de%20Microsoft%C2%A0Copilot%20Chat/Playbook_BatchProcessing_v1-1-0.md)

---

## Procesos No Repetibles

### Incluyen

- Facturación
- Liquidaciones
- Cierres contables
- Sincronización crítica
- Actualizaciones sensibles

### Estrategias

- Rollback completo
- Rollback por Step
- Recuperación controlada

```mermaid
flowchart LR
A[Falla]
--> B[Rollback]
--> C[Recuperación]

1

docs.title: Entrada
docs.text: Datos desde BD

docs.title: Flujo
docs.text: text Reader → Writer

docs.title: Salida
docs.text:
• CSV
  • Excel
  • PDF

docs.title: Tipo
docs.text: Repetible

docs.title: Entrada
docs.text: Millones de registros

docs.title: Flujo
docs.text: text Reader → Processor → Writer

docs.title: Salida
docs.text: Actualización masiva

docs.title: Tipo
docs.text: Chunk Processing

docs.title: Entrada
docs.text: Base de datos

docs.title: Flujo
docs.text: text Reader → Processor → Kafka Writer

docs.title: Salida
docs.text: Eventos

docs.title: Tipo
docs.text: Utilería

docs.title: Entrada
docs.text: Movimientos financieros

docs.title: Flujo
docs.text: text Reader → Reglas → Writer

docs.title: Salida
docs.text: Facturas

docs.title: Tipo
docs.text:
No repetible

1

docs.title: 🧩 Metodología de Desarrollo
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Análisis]
--> B[Identificar reglas]
--> C[Diseñar Steps]
--> D[Desarrollar]
--> E[Pruebas]
--> F[Despliegue]

docs.title: Prerrequisitos
docs.text:
• Entendimiento funcional
  • Volumetría esperada
  • Dependencias
  • Secuencia de ejecución

Participantes mínimos:

  • Backend Developer
  • Technical Lead
  • Responsable de Negocio1

docs.title: Tecnologías
docs.text: Tecnología Versión Java 21 Maven 3.2.3+ Spring Batch 5.1

docs.title: Estructura de Proyecto
docs.text:
```text config/ config.repository/

constant/ dto/ exception/

listener/ listener.impl/

repository/ repository.impl/ repository.mapper/

step.reader/ step.processor/ step.writer/ step.tasklet/

util/ util.impl/ ```1

docs.title: Propiedades Obligatorias
docs.text: properties batch.env batch.grid.size batch.block.size batch.chunk.size batch.skip.limit1

docs.title: 🔧 Componentes Principales de Spring Batch
docs.text: Componente Función Job Proceso completo Step Etapa del Job ItemReader Lectura ItemProcessor Transformación ItemWriter Escritura Chunk Bloque transaccional Listener Observación JobRepository Persistencia ExecutionContext Persistencia de contexto

docs.title: Chunk Processing (Estándar)
docs.text:
```mermaid flowchart LR

A[Reader] --> B[Chunk] --> C[Processor] --> D[Writer] ```

docs.title: Beneficios
docs.text:
• Menor uso de conexiones
  • Mejor control transaccional
  • Mayor rendimiento
  • Mejor control de errores1

docs.title: Problema
docs.text: Alto consumo de memoria.

docs.title: Solución
docs.text:
Dividir la información en páginas.

```text 10,000 registros

Página 1 = 2,000 Página 2 = 2,000 Página 3 = 2,000 Página 4 = 2,000 Página 5 = 2,000 ```1

docs.title: Problema
docs.text: Procesamiento lento.

docs.title: Solución
docs.text:
Ejecutar particiones en paralelo.

```mermaid flowchart TD

MASTER[Master Step]

MASTER --> P1[Partition 1] MASTER --> P2[Partition 2] MASTER --> P3[Partition 3] MASTER --> P4[Partition 4] ```1

docs.title: Problema
docs.text: Errores recuperables.

docs.title: Solución
docs.text:
Definir políticas de:

  • Retry
  • Skip
  • Error Fatal

```mermaid flowchart LR

A[Excepción]

A --> B{Recuperable?}

B -->|Si| C[Retry]

B -->|No| D[Skip]

D --> E[Continuar] ```

1

docs.title: Chunk
docs.text: Parámetro Valor Chunk mínimo 2500 Chunk máximo 5000

docs.title: Fetch Size
docs.text: Parámetro Valor Fetch Size 1500 - 5000

docs.title: Pool de Conexiones
docs.text: Parámetro Valor Mínimo 5 Máximo 50

docs.title: Hilos
docs.text: text No exceder los núcleos disponibles del CPU.1

docs.title: NO HACER
docs.text:
• Consultas a Base de Datos
  • Consumo de APIs
  • Consumo de Kafka
  • Consumo de Microservicios

docs.title: Motivo
docs.text:
El Processor se ejecuta por cada registro.

text 1 millón de registros = 1 millón de llamadas

El Processor únicamente debe transformar información. 1

docs.title: JobListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Inicio
  • Fin
  • Métricas

docs.title: StepExecutionListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Inicio de Step
  • Fin de Step

docs.title: ItemReadListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Lecturas
  • Errores de lectura

docs.title: ItemProcessListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Transformaciones
  • Errores de proceso

docs.title: ItemWriteListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Escritura
  • Errores de persistencia

docs.title: ChunkListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Commit
  • Rollback

docs.title: SkipListener
docs.text:
Observa:

  • Registros descartados1

docs.title: Procesos Repetibles
docs.text: java @Transactional

docs.title: Procesos Críticos
docs.text:
java @Transactional( propagation = Propagation.REQUIRES_NEW )

1

docs.title: Recuperación de Errores
docs.text:
Todo proceso debe definir:

text Retry Skip Rollback Restart1

docs.title: Metadata Obligatoria
docs.text:
Spring Batch debe persistir:

text BATCH_JOB_INSTANCE BATCH_JOB_EXECUTION BATCH_STEP_EXECUTION BATCH_JOB_EXECUTION_CONTEXT BATCH_STEP_EXECUTION_CONTEXT

Esto permite:

  • Reinicios
  • Auditoría
  • Recuperación
  • Monitoreo1

docs.title: Procesamiento Paralelo
docs.text:
Utilizar:

java ThreadPoolTaskExecutor

y

java Partitioning

cuando el proceso lo permita.1

docs.title: ❌ Anti-Patrones
docs.text: Anti-Patrón Consecuencia SELECT * Sobreconsumo Chunk enorme OutOfMemory Processor consumiendo servicios Bajo rendimiento Sin Metadata No recuperable Sin Listeners Sin trazabilidad Jobs no idempotentes Duplicidad Reintentos infinitos Saturación Sin Partitioning Bajo desempeño

docs.title: ✅ Checklist de Cumplimiento
docs.text: Validación Obligatorio Idempotencia ✅ Metadata Spring Batch ✅ Reader Processor Writer ✅ Chunk Processing ✅ Retry y Skip ✅ Manejo de excepciones ✅ Monitoreo ✅ Listener ✅ Control transaccional ✅ Pruebas de estrés ✅ Pruebas de volumetría ✅ Modularización ✅ Uso eficiente de BD ✅ Paralelización cuando aplique ✅

docs.title: 🎯 Los 5 Pilares del Estándar Batch
docs.text:
mindmap
  root((Batch Processing))
    Idempotencia
    Escalabilidad
    Observabilidad
    Recuperación de errores
    Rendimiento

docs.title: Regla Final
docs.text:
Si un Batch cumple:

  • Idempotencia
  • Monitoreo
  • Metadata
  • Retry & Skip
  • Chunk Processing
  • Separación Reader / Processor / Writer
  • Control Transaccional
  • Recuperación ante Fallos

entonces está alineado con el estándar definido en el Playbook corporativo. 1
